text:
There once was an apple orchard nestled amongst trees that whispered secrets untold. Among the crowd of vibrant hues hung apples ripe for picking. One day, an old crow named Old Wisdom flew low across rows old friends.

"You've grown beautiful beauties. Time never weighs down feather."

Old Wisdom swooped down into an apple tree she lovingly named Oakheart. Old Wisdom was respected for her wisdom beyond her feathers. Old Wisdom couldn³ just talk; she felt something profound she couldn›;t share.

She thought about a young dove named Dawnflower that had flown southward seeking warmer shores. Old Wisdom thought of Dawnflower's maiden saillocks fluttering gently. Old Wisdom felt an ache deep within her heart.

Her feather hearts aching, her feathers shimmering with sorrowful tears:

"Oh young doveling... You wouldn³ just love an oak? Perhaps oak loves you more."

Old Wisdom couldnít fly away. Instead, she sat on a branch feeling a warm glow inside. Old Wisdom sang
